metadata.title:
MARTYRS MORE.
NUMEROUS

RANK AND FILE OF I. W. w.
ACCEPT IMPRISONMENT AND
JAIL FARE THEIR LEADER
DODGEO.

pages.raw_text:
Nineteen more martyrs to free
speech" enthusiastically broke into
the city bastile yesterday after
noon and evening by attempting to
harangue crowds within the fire
limits. All of them are from the
ranks of the Industrial Workers of
the World. They cursed and shout
ed most of the night in celebration
of the event. This morning W..
Smith, e. r. Chaplin, Charley
Grant. w. Clare, Nick Foskimp and
A. Kruger, convicted in police
court Saturday of speaking on the
streets and sentenced by Judge
Mann to 80 days in Jail and fined
500 and costs each, refused to
work on the rock pile and will be
confined on diet of bread and
water.


Though Organizer Walsh of the
l. w. w. was able to buy ham and
eggs and other dainties not in
cluded in the plain prison menu,
and also able to secure bail, not
one of the parties arrested yester-
day had the price of coffee and
dOughnuts when booked at the
police station. Only a few of them
can speak the English language in
diligently.


Two women with gaudy red
scarfs wound about their threats
mingled with the throngs. They
were a portion of the squad Of
women speakers mentioned by
Walsh the other day, but they re.
fused to mount the box after 10 of
the male martyrs had been taken
by the police.


The police have prepared for
drastic measures in case of VIC
lence on the part of the violators
of the ordinance against street
speaking inside the fire limits.
